steps.title: Follow the consequence
steps.content: The previous sentence explains that Roman numerals show the sequence of numerical designations, not a moon's distance from Jupiter. The next sentence draws a conclusion from that rule: it is wrong to assume Helike is the forty-fifth moon by proximity.

steps.title: Reject other transitions
steps.content: Choice A signals contrast, but the idea follows from the previous sentence. Choice B signals the last item in a list, and no list is being completed. Choice D adds another point rather than marking a conclusion. A consequence transition fits. The answer is C.
